Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Interface IFrameGenerator

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Interfaces
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Coordinates the generation of video frames from Apple IIe system state.

public interface IFrameGenerator

Remarks

This interface is responsible for orchestrating the frame rendering process by:

  • Allocating render contexts with necessary dependencies (memory, status, frame buffer)
  • Invoking the appropriate renderer to convert video memory into displayable pixels
  • Managing the rendering pipeline from system state to final frame buffer

The frame generator acts as a coordinator between the emulator core (which provides system state and memory) and the renderer (which produces pixels). It abstracts the details of render context creation and renderer invocation, providing a simple interface for the UI or display subsystem to request rendered frames.

Typical usage pattern: 

var context = frameGenerator.AllocateRenderContext();
frameGenerator.RenderFrame(context);
// context.FrameBuffer now contains the rendered frame

Methods

AllocateRenderContext()

Allocates and initializes a render context for frame generation.

RenderContext AllocateRenderContext()

Returns

RenderContext

A RenderContext containing the frame buffer, memory access, and system status required for rendering a frame.

Remarks

This method creates a render context that packages together all the dependencies needed for frame rendering:

  • Frame buffer - The writable bitmap where pixels will be rendered
  • Memory access - Direct access to video memory (main and auxiliary)
  • System status - Current soft switch states (video mode, page selection, etc.)

The allocated context is typically short-lived, used for rendering a single frame and then discarded. For performance, the underlying frame buffer may be reused through a double-buffering mechanism managed by the IFrameProvider.

This method should be called once per frame, typically in response to a VBlank event or refresh timer tick (~60 Hz).

RenderFrame(RenderContext)

Renders the current Apple IIe video state into the provided context's frame buffer.

void RenderFrame(RenderContext context)

Parameters

context RenderContext

The RenderContext containing the frame buffer, memory access, and system status. Typically obtained from AllocateRenderContext(). As a reference type, the context's invalidation state is shared across all references.

Remarks

This method invokes the configured renderer (typically IDisplayBitmapRenderer) to convert the current video memory contents and mode settings into a displayable bitmap. The rendering process:

  1. Examines the system status to determine active video mode (text/graphics, mixed, etc.)
  2. Reads video memory from the appropriate pages (main/auxiliary, page 1/2)
  3. Applies character ROM lookups for text modes or color generation for graphics modes
  4. Writes the resulting pixels to the context's frame buffer
  5. Invalidates the context to prevent reuse (implementation detail)

This method should complete quickly (ideally under 16ms for 60 fps) to maintain smooth frame rates. The actual rendering work is delegated to the renderer implementation, which may employ various optimizations (dirty rectangle tracking, scanline caching, etc.).

Context Lifetime: After this method completes, the provided context is invalidated and cannot be reused. Attempting to access properties or call methods on the context after this method returns will throw InvalidOperationException. Allocate a new context for each frame via AllocateRenderContext().
